items.q: How do I install a pack?
items.a: Drop the pack folder into BepInEx/dinoforge_packs/, then press F10 in-game to enable it. The pack must have a valid pack.yaml manifest.
Packs are installed in BepInEx/dinoforge_packs/ subfolder. Each pack is a separate folder with its own pack.yaml, YAML/JSON content files, and optional assets.
A total conversion is a pack that replaces core game content (factions, units, buildings) rather than adding to it. Mark your pack as type: total_conversion in pack.yaml.

items.q: What's the difference between a mod and a pack?
items.a: In DINOForge, packs ARE mods. Every mod is a pack with a pack.yaml manifest, content files, and optional dependencies and conflict rules.

items.q: Can I enable multiple packs at once?
items.a: Yes. DINOForge detects conflicts and will warn you if packs overlap. Enable/disable packs from the F10 mod menu.
Opens the debug overlay. Shows FPS, garbage collection stats, loaded entity count, active packs, and memory usage. Press F9 again to close.
Opens the mod menu. Enable/disable packs, view pack metadata, see dependencies and conflicts, configure per-pack settings.
Closes the current panel. If debug overlay (F9) is open, Esc closes it. If mod menu (F10) is open, Esc closes it.
Hot-reload packs. Reloads all pack YAML files without restarting the game. Use after editing pack content locally.

items.q: Mod not showing up in F10?
items.a: Check: (1) Pack folder is in BepInEx/dinoforge_packs/. (2) pack.yaml exists and is valid YAML. (3) Run 'dinoforge verify-pack <path>' to validate structure.
This usually means a pack has invalid content. Disable all packs via F10, then enable them one at a time to isolate the problem. Check BepInEx/LogOutput.log for errors.
Asset swaps require valid asset bundle files in packs/*/assets/bundles/. Check F9 debug overlay to verify the pack loaded. Check BepInEx/dinoforge_debug.log for asset swap errors.

items.q: Pack loads but has no content?
items.a: Your pack.yaml is valid, but the content files (units.yaml, factions.yaml, etc) may have errors. Run 'dinoforge verify-pack <path>' to check for schema violations.
Ensure BepInEx/plugins/DINOForge.Runtime.dll exists. Check BepInEx/LogOutput.log for load errors. Re-run the DINOForge installer or deploy manually via 'dotnet build -p:DeployToGame=true'.
Hot Module Reload (HMR) lets you edit pack YAML files and press Ctrl+R in-game to reload without restarting. Use for rapid iteration on balance changes and content.

items.q: How do I write a pack from scratch?
items.a: Use 'dinoforge new <pack-name>' to scaffold a pack. Or copy the example-balance pack as a template. Edit pack.yaml, then add units.yaml, factions.yaml, buildings.yaml, etc.
The DINOForge SDK is a C# NuGet package for writing plugins and custom content loaders in code (not YAML). See docs at kooshapari.github.io/Dino/sdk.

items.q: How do I report a bug?
items.a: Open an issue at github.com/KooshaPari/Dino/issues. Include game version, BepInEx version, loaded packs (from F9), and BepInEx/LogOutput.log output.

items.q: What's the best way to debug a pack?
items.a: Use 'dinoforge verify-pack <path>' to check structure. Check BepInEx/LogOutput.log and BepInEx/dinoforge_debug.log for errors. Use F9 debug overlay to inspect loaded data.
